steps.title: Rearrange and identify coefficients
steps.content: Start with $2x^2 - 4x = t$ and move $t$ to the left side to get the standard quadratic form $2x^2 - 4x - t = 0$. Here $a = 2$, $b = -4$, and $c = -t$.

steps.title: Apply the no-real-solutions condition
steps.content:
A quadratic has no real solutions exactly when its discriminant $b^2 - 4ac$ is negative. Substitute the coefficients:
$$(-4)^2 - 4(2)(-t) \lt 0$$
$$16 + 8t \lt 0$$
$$t \lt -2$$

steps.title: Test each choice against $t \lt -2$
steps.content:
• A) $t = -3$: is $-3 \lt -2$? Yes — no real solutions.
  • B) $t = -1$: is $-1 \lt -2$? No — eliminate.
  • C) $t = 1$: is $1 \lt -2$? No — eliminate.
  • D) $t = 3$: is $3 \lt -2$? No — eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
